wins: Delivered year-over-year operating profit growth and margin expansion in every quarter of FY '24
Lower CapEx and higher free cash flow, returning nearly $4 billion to stockholders
Meaningful improvement in return on invested capital
Achieved $1.8 billion in structural cost-out in FY '24
Expected to deliver adjusted EPS growth of 12% to 24% in FY '25
risks: Decline in revenue compared to initial growth expectations
International yield pressures from tapering export demand surcharges
Headwinds from the U.S. Postal Service contract termination
Uncertainty around the impact of trade policy changes on China e-commerce volumes
Potential network disruption from Network 2.0 integration
